one_sentence: Dense captures meaning, sparse captures exact tokens; hybrid wins when both matter — but only fuse when traffic actually carries exact-match queries, and tune the blend per query type or hybrid loses to dense.

operation_candidates.name: WhenHybridChecklist
operation_candidates.trigger: Deciding whether to add sparse to a dense pipeline.
action: Sample >=50 real queries; label semantic / lexical / mixed.
Compute lexical share.
Confirm corpus has exact-match tokens AND queries reference them AND those tokens appear verbatim in chunks.
<5% -> dense-only; 5-50% -> hybrid; >50% -> invert to sparse-first.
operation_candidates.output: Go/no-go with lexical share recorded; 'no' is a valid common outcome.
evidence: llamaindex: Dilemma 2 decision steps; OP-04 (trigger is traffic-driven, not theoretical)

operation_candidates.name: ChooseFusionMethod
operation_candidates.trigger: Two legs wired; need to combine their result lists.
action: Default RRF (reciprocal_rerank): rank-based, robust to dense-cosine vs BM25 score-scale gap.
Use weighted/relative-score only with a labeled set to fit weights and normalized scores.
Never naive-sum un-normalized scores.
operation_candidates.output: One deliberate fusion method plus the reason chosen.

operation_candidates.name: WireBM25Leg
operation_candidates.trigger: Hybrid approved; sparse leg not yet built.
action: BM25Retriever.from_defaults(nodes=...) over the SAME node set as the dense index.
Persist/rebuild the BM25 index as a deployment artifact alongside the vector index.
Set per-leg similarity_top_k wide (10-20).
operation_candidates.output: Sparse retriever consistent with the dense index, returning enough candidates to fuse.

operation_candidates.name: PreferNativeHybrid
operation_candidates.trigger: The vector store supports sparse internally.
action: Use store-native hybrid: Qdrant sparse vectors + FusionQuery, Weaviate hybrid(alpha=), Pinecone sparse-dense, pgvector full-text + vector, Milvus RRFRanker/WeightedRanker.
One round-trip, one consistency domain, no separate BM25 index to keep in sync.
operation_candidates.output: Hybrid with no second index to keep in sync; lower operational surface.
evidence: llamaindex: OP-04 (or vendor hybrid Qdrant/Milvus alpha)

operation_candidates.name: AlphaTunePerType
operation_candidates.trigger: Fusion wired; recall not yet optimized; or hybrid 'loses to dense'.
action: Evaluate alpha/weights at {0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1.0} on labeled subsets PER query type, not globally.
Lexical -> low alpha (sparse-leaning); semantic -> high alpha (dense-leaning).
operation_candidates.output: Per-type alpha curve; the alpha that lifts lexical without regressing semantic.
evidence: llamaindex: Dilemma 2 (tune per type, not globally)

operation_candidates.name: RouteThenAlpha
operation_candidates.trigger: No single global alpha satisfies both query types without regression.
action: Classify query type first.
Dispatch to a retriever configured with the per-type alpha (or pure dense / pure sparse).
Hand classification to a router (query-routing) if available.
operation_candidates.output: Each query gets its optimal blend; semantic and lexical slices both peak.

operation_candidates.name: InvertForLexicalCorpus
operation_candidates.trigger: Lexical share >50% (code search, log search, legal/medical citation lookup).
action: Make BM25 the primary retriever.
Use dense as a fallback / rerank signal to catch paraphrase, not as the lead leg.
Equivalent to alpha pinned low.
operation_candidates.output: Recall dominated by exact-token matching where users want it; dense recovers the semantic minority.
evidence: llamaindex: Dilemma 2 step 4 (BM25-first, dense as fallback rerank)

operation_candidates.name: HybridEvalGate
action: Run the eval loop on BOTH slices.
Assert lexical-slice recall/MRR rises vs dense baseline.
Assert semantic-slice metrics do not regress.
Gate merge on both.
operation_candidates.output: Quantitative proof hybrid helped where intended and harmed nothing else.
evidence: llamaindex: Stage 2 eval loop (gate every change)

dilemmas.title: Hybrid (BM25 + dense) vs pure dense: worth the complexity?
dilemmas.resolution: Traffic-driven: <5% lexical -> dense-only; 5-50% -> hybrid with per-type alpha; >50% -> invert to BM25-first. Hybrid lifts the lexical slice with no semantic degradation IF alpha is tuned per type.

dilemmas.title: Alpha for keyword-heavy vs semantic queries: one knob, two demands
dilemmas.resolution: A single global alpha lands in a valley that underperforms pure dense on the semantic majority. Tune alpha per query type; if no global alpha works, route by type and apply per-route alpha. For >50% lexical corpora, invert.

anti_patterns: A1 hybrid by default on a purely semantic corpus
A2 single global alpha for mixed traffic
A3 ignoring fusion method / naive-summing incomparable scores
A4 BM25 leg over a different/stale node set
A5 'semantic dropped so hybrid is bad' (alpha was global)
A6 final cut taken before fusion (no candidate headroom)
A7 separate BM25 index when native hybrid exists
A8 hybrid as substitute for reranker
A9 shipping hybrid with no per-slice eval
extraction_note: Standalone overlay extracted from llamaindex SKILL.md Stage 3 step 4 (hybrid search) + OP-04 AddHybridBM25 + references/R3-dilemma-cases.md Dilemma 2 (Hybrid BM25+dense vs pure dense, alpha-tuning per query type). Frontmatter style aligned to ~/.claude/skills/llamaindex.
